description_en: Vigorous exercises, such as swimming or walking, designed to strengthen the heart and lungs.
example: She goes to an aerobics class three times a week.

description_en: A person who is proficient in sports and other forms of physical exercise.
example: Olympic athletes train for years to compete at the highest level.

description_en: A game with rackets in which a shuttlecock is played back and forth across a net.
example: Badminton is a very popular sport in Southeast Asia.

meaning: chức vô địch; giải vô địch
description_en: A contest for the position of champion in a sport or game.
example: The team won the world championship for the third time in a row.

description_en: The practice of training people to obey rules or a code of behavior.
example: Success in professional sports requires a great deal of self-discipline.

description_en: The fact or power of enduring an unpleasant or difficult process or situation without giving way.
example: Long-distance running is a test of physical and mental endurance.
note: Ability to sustain effort over time.

description_en: The condition of being physically fit and healthy.
example: She goes to the gym regularly to maintain her physical fitness.

description_en: Exercises developing or displaying physical agility and coordination.
example: Gymnastics requires incredible flexibility and strength.
note: Includes bars, beam, and floor exercises.

description_en: An upright frame, typically one of a series, that athletes in a race must jump over.
example: He fell at the final hurdle and lost the lead.
note: Obstacle in a race; also used metaphorically.

description_en: An instance of being injured.
example: A serious knee injury ended his football career.
note: Accident or pain caused by physical activity.

description_en: A light spear thrown in a competitive sport or as a weapon.
example: She won a silver medal in the javelin throw.

meaning: cú đá khởi đầu; sự bắt đầu
description_en: A kick of the ball that starts play in a football or rugby match.
example: The kickoff is scheduled for 3 p.m.

description_en: A collection of people, countries, or groups that combine for a particular purpose, typically mutual protection or cooperation.
example: The Premier League is the top level of the English football league system.

description_en: A long-distance running race, strictly one of 26 miles 385 yards (42.195 km).
example: Running a marathon requires months of intense preparation.
note: Named after the historic Greek event.

description_en: Efficient as food; nourishing.
example: Athletes need a balanced and nutritious diet to perform well.
note: Providing the necessary food for health.

description_en: Someone who competes against or fights another in a contest, game, or argument.
example: The reigning champion proved to be a tough opponent.
note: The person you are competing against.

description_en: The form, size, and development of a person's body.
example: Bodybuilders work hard to achieve a muscular physique.

description_en: A match or contest that a person or team must win in order to be eligible for a later part of a competition.
example: The national team is playing in the World Cup qualifiers.
note: Match that determines entry to a tournament.

description_en: An official who watches a game or match closely to ensure that the rules are adhered to and to settle disputes arising from the play.
example: The referee blew the whistle to end the match.
note: Official in charge of a game.

description_en: A sports arena with tiers of seats for spectators.
example: The new stadium can hold over 50,000 fans.

description_en: A series of contests between a number of competitors, who compete for an overall prize.
example: The tennis tournament is held every summer in Wimbledon.

description_en: An official who watches a game or match closely to enforce the rules and settle disputes.
example: The tennis umpire called the ball out.
note: Specific title for officials in certain sports.

description_en: An act of defeating an enemy or opponent in a battle, game, or other competition.
example: The team celebrated their hard-earned victory in the finals.

description_en: A session of vigorous physical exercise or training.
example: A morning workout is a great way to start the day.

description_en: A gymnasium.
example: I'm heading to the gym to lift some weights.

description_en: A Hindu spiritual and ascetic discipline, including breath control, simple meditation, and the adoption of specific bodily postures.
example: Yoga helps improve both flexibility and mental focus.
note: Focuses on Mind and body connection.

description_en: Great energy or enthusiasm in pursuit of a cause or an objective.
example: The young athlete approached her training with incredible zeal.

description_en: Ability to move quickly and easily.
example: The goalkeeper showed great agility in saving the penalty shot.

description_en: A game played between two teams of five players in which goals are scored by throwing a ball through a netted hoop.
example: Basketball is one of the Most popular sports in the US.
note: Played on a court with a hoop.

description_en: The sport or activity of riding a bicycle.
example: Cycling is a low-impact exercise that is good for the joints.

description_en: The action of defending from or resisting attack.
example: The team has a very strong defense that is hard to break through.
note: Strategy to prevent the opponent from scoring.

description_en: Physical or mental effort.
example: Continuous physical exertion over three hours can lead to dehydration.

description_en: A game played by two teams of eleven players using a round ball which players kick up and down the field.
example: Football is known as 'the beautiful game' in many countries.
note: Known as soccer in some regions.

description_en: The player whose task is to stop the ball from going into the goal.
example: The goalkeeper made a spectacular save in the last minute of the game.
note: Stays in front of the goal.

description_en: A game similar to football in which the ball is thrown and caught with the hands.
example: Handball is a fast-paced and physically demanding sport.

description_en: A pause or break in activity.
example: Interval training involving short bursts of intense exercise is very effective.
note: Alternating periods of work and rest.

description_en: A sport of Japanese origin in which the objective is to throw or tackle an opponent to the ground.
example: He has been practicing judo since he was seven years old.

description_en: A martial art developed in the Ryukyu Islands in which the hands and feet are used as weapons.
example: She recently earned her black belt in karate.

description_en: One circuit of a track or racecourse.
example: The swimmer completed fifty laps in the Olympic-sized pool.
note: One full round of a course.

description_en: The reason or reasons one has for acting or behaving in a particular way.
example: Staying healthy is his primary motivation for exercising regularly.

description_en: The structure of mesh that divides the court in sports like tennis or volleyball.
example: The volleyball hit the top of the net and fell onto the other side.

description_en: The action of attacking an opponent's goal or territory.
example: The team decided to switch to a more aggressive offense to catch up.

description_en: An area of ground marked out or used for playing a game.
example: The players ran onto the pitch as the fans cheered.
note: Specific name for a sports field.

description_en: A bat with a round or oval frame strung with catgut, nylon, etc., used in tennis, badminton, or squash.
example: You need a high-quality tennis racket to play well.

description_en: The action or sport of moving on skates.
example: Ice skating is a popular winter activity for families.
note: Includes ice, roller, and inline skating.

description_en: An action or strategy carefully planned to achieve a specific end.
example: The coach spent hours analyzing the opponent's game tactics.

description_en: Not able to be defeated or bettered.
example: The team has been virtually unbeatable this entire season.

description_en: A game for two teams of six players, in which a large ball is hit by hand over a high net.
example: Beach volleyball is a fun sport to play with friends.
note: Played by hitting the ball over a net.

description_en: A period of preparatory exercise.
example: Proper warm-up is essential to prevent injuries during training.
note: Light physical activity before intense work.

description_en: The period between childhood and adult age.
example: The youth development program aims to find future Olympic stars.
note: Early stage of an athlete's career.
